summary: This is a lengthy mentorship/podcast interview focused on Joey Batista's personal philosophy, business journey, and approach to coaching/mentorship. While Batista discusses past real estate deals and business principles broadly, the content centers on mindset, giving back, family values, and building a coaching brand rather than specific deal analysis, sourcing tactics, or underwriting methodology relevant to Brian's 15-50 unit multifamily niche.
takeaways: Use seller financing by offering slightly above market price to unlock zero-down deals with operational runway (paid $350k over 39 months at $3,500/month vs. $300k cash for property that became $2.25M value)
'Portray weakness to gain strength' - Present humility and limited resources to increase counterparty's willingness to negotiate favorable terms
Best fight of your life is not to get more, but to keep what you got - Protection mode is critical in business cycles
Mastermind moderator quality matters more than attendees - value transfer and net worth growth per member annually indicates effectiveness
Build brand as talent magnet - long-form content signals values to attract aligned team members who won't burn out after 2-3 years
Develop people, not just tasks - 80% of entrepreneurs are solopreneurs; become entrepreneur by building delegatable team
When facing adversity, shift immediately from weakness/sorrow state to powerful action state - use music/barefoot grounding to reset energy
title: Why Does Helping Others Feel So Good?
